title: Refactor OCP-032 (OCP): Switch statement on JsonToken ID requires modification to support new token t...

detected_issue.description: Switch statement on JsonToken ID requires modification to support new token types.
detected_issue.reasoning: The switch statement on `_currToken.id()` necessitates changes to existing code whenever new token types are introduced in the Jackson framework.

fix_logic.summary: Replaced switch statement in `getStringCharacters` with an EnumMap for OCP adherence.
fix_logic.rationale: The original `getStringCharacters` method contained a switch statement on `_currToken.id()`, which violates the Open/Closed Principle (OCP) because adding new `JsonToken` types would necessitate modifying this method. The refactoring replaces the switch statement with a static `EnumMap` that maps `JsonToken` instances to `TokenCharGetter` functional interfaces. This approach centralizes the token-specific logic into the map's initialization block. Now, `getStringCharacters` is closed for modification; if a new `JsonToken` requires special handling, only the `TOKEN_CHAR_GETTERS` map needs to be updated, rather than altering the method's core logic. For tokens not explicitly in the map, the default `_currToken.asCharArray()` is used, leveraging existing polymorphism.
